# Questions in Livestream 151

Live Stream #151: Nine Myths About Spam

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:16</a> | Could gods like Osiris or Rama be based on real people later deified? | He leans more toward historical people being mythologized than toward an Egyptian divine-council explanation, while leaving room for uncertainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:03</a> | What does the Mount Ebal cultic site add to the case for the Exodus? | He says it fits a late-conquest chronology well and is hard to dismiss as anything other than a cult site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=1135" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:55</a> | How can someone resist Kabbalistic thinking when it seems appealing after converting to Christianity? | He recommends becoming deeply familiar with the Bible so counterfeit or extra-biblical ideas are easier to recognize. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=1523" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:23</a> | Did ancient Egyptian magic have a concept like mana or a limited energy supply, and how would it be replenished? | He says Egyptian magic did not work like mana; it was mostly sympathetic magic, with success thought to depend on alignment with the natural order. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=1841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:41</a> | What was the Egyptian title `kheret nesut`, and why did it emerge? | He describes it as a title for high-status court women or ladies in waiting, but says its exact origin and role are uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2074" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:34</a> | What music do you listen to, if any? | He says his tastes are eclectic, including soundtracks, alternative music, anime music, and other styles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:29</a> | Does "spam" here mean the meat or Ron Wyatt fans? | He says the title joke means the meat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2602" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:22</a> | What are the flaws of purity culture? | He says purity culture had legitimate concerns but often handled sexuality in unhealthy or excessive ways. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=2980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:40</a> | Did Egyptians need to be buried in Egyptian soil to reach the afterlife? | He says no; burial in foreign territory did not automatically block the afterlife. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=3187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:07</a> | Can Christians celebrate nature, and what about non-demonic nature spirits? | He says nature can be celebrated but praise belongs to God, and any good spirit would redirect attention back to God rather than to itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=3583" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:43</a> | Is Mark 2:26 wrong when Jesus says Abiathar was high priest when David ate the showbread? | He says Abiathar was not high priest at the moment but became the major priestly figure associated with the episode and its aftermath. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:36</a> | When in Ramesses II's reign would the Exodus occur, and why would Egypt not keep chasing Israel? | He places it roughly around the king's twenty-fifth regnal year and says Egypt was too reduced, especially in chariot strength, to chase them across Sinai. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:45</a> | What is one of your guilty pleasures? | He says taking a day off, because running the channel leaves little real downtime. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:08</a> | What are your general thoughts on Edgar Allan Poe? | He calls Poe an excellent writer with a strange life and death, and mentions liking his work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=4683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:03</a> | What are your thoughts on Jean-Paul Sartre and Ernest Hemingway? | He regards both as good writers, while noting Sartre's atheism and late-life conversion claims in the discussion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=5121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:21</a> | What are your thoughts on H. P. Lovecraft? | He says Lovecraft was very good at creating atmosphere and mood, which he sees as central to Lovecraft's fiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=5233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:13</a> | Can you provide commentary on Romans 7:14-25? | He summarizes the passage as Paul describing bondage to sin, the goodness of the law, and the need for rescue through Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=5959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:19</a> | Are you going to watch `Testament: The Story of Moses`? | He says he might, but had not heard of it until the question was raised. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=5996" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:56</a> | What do you mean by the Torah as Moses's memoir? | He compares it to day-by-day court writing later compiled into a single work by an editor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6203" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:23</a> | Why was it bad to remove the poles from the ark in Numbers 4:5-6? | He points to Exodus 25:15, where the poles are commanded to remain in the ark's rings, while saying the exact reason is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6358" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:58</a> | How could Jephthah have escaped his vow? | He points to Leviticus 27 as a redemption provision for an extreme or hasty vow. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6697" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:37</a> | What is an executive summary? | He defines it as reducing a larger report or concept to a short paragraph for someone who does not have time to read the full material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=6786" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:06</a> | How would Egyptians smack-talk or insult people? | He discusses Egyptian pejoratives and says some insults were humorous, but the transcript only gives a partial survey. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=7314" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:54</a> | How do we know Numbers 12:3 is not a later self-praising insertion? | He says the wording does not read like ordinary self-praise and fits the narrative style rather than a simple boast. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=7507" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:07</a> | How can tarot cards be used to teach the gospel? | He says early tarot cards appear to have used ordered biblical imagery before later divinatory uses developed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=7810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:10</a> | What is your verdict on the filioque? | He rejects the filioque but says he also rejects the Eastern Orthodox reasoning based on eternal generation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=8034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:54</a> | Why is Calvinism not correct? | He says he is a three-point Calvinist, thinks Calvinism is more correct than Arminianism in some respects, but rejects some modern Calvinist formulations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=8350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:10</a> | How should 1 John 2:27 and 2 John 1:9-11 be understood together? | He says the received gospel is sufficient and true, and false teachers with a different gospel should not be allowed to teach the congregation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=8779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:19</a> | What is an example of Jesus being funny in the Greek? | He points to Jesus asking "what things?" on the Emmaus road and to the insult of calling Herod a fox as examples with humorous force. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:31</a> | Is it biblical to suggest that the ends justify the means? | He says no; the means must justify themselves, and the ends-justify-the-means approach leads to unbiblical ethics. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:51</a> | Why distinguish between a christophany and a theophany? | He says the terms overlap but can be distinguished when identifying whether the appearance is specifically of Christ or more broadly of God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:21</a> | What helps the channel more, patrons or YouTube members? | He says both help in different ways: Patreon gives more net financial support, while YouTube members tend to be more engaged in the channel community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:10</a> | When is the next Dungeons & Dragons game? | He says probably next November unless someone has a strong reason to schedule one sooner. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=9999" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:39</a> | What is your favorite Exodus movie, and why? | He names the 1950s Cecil B. DeMille film for its Egyptian archaeological detail, while criticizing the long romance section. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=10229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:29</a> | Could the blocked movie commentary be put on Patreon, Dailymotion, Vimeo, or Rumble? | He says no; those platforms also face copyright enforcement, and Patreon can freeze accounts for copyrighted material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=10451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:11</a> | What advice would you give for starting a church class on physical evidence for the Bible? | He recommends using reliable scholarly materials and being cautious with internet apologetics sources that handle evidence poorly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=10648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:28</a> | What are your thoughts on Genesis 3 being the fall of Satan? | He thinks the reading is viable and connects it with Satan's expulsion and his own discussion of Satan as a covering cherub. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=10741" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:01</a> | What are your thoughts on Isaiah 65:17 and memory in heaven? | He says the point is not that people forget former things, but that former troubles no longer matter in a healed state. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=11056" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:16</a> | Do you accept late exilic authorship of Genesis 1-11? | He says no; the theory would not be devastating if true, but he does not find the evidence or arguments compelling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=11265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:45</a> | What is meant in 2 Peter 2:11? | He explains the passage as saying judgment belongs to God, not even to righteous angelic powers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=11671" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:31</a> | Did Israel learn household codes, gender roles, business practice, and similar laws from its history in Egypt? | He says Israelite law formation drew on multiple sources, including wilderness case law, Egypt, Mesopotamia, and local Canaanite contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=11804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:44</a> | What ancient Near Eastern background helps with sacrifices and holiness in Leviticus? | He mentions priestly files, Hittite festival parallels, Egyptian purification practices, and the need for more source-based commentaries. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=12112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:52</a> | Do Israelites come from Canaanites, and are Amorites the same as Canaanites? | He says no; he identifies Israel's ancestry with Amorites and says Amorites were northern Mesopotamians, not Canaanites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=12247" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:07</a> | Why are the Maccabees and the rest of the Apocrypha not in the canon? | He frames the issue as canon-law history, says Luther did not newly exclude them, and cites historical and authorship problems as barriers to full canonicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=12660" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:00</a> | What are your thoughts on Denis Villeneuve's newer Dune films, and which Dune adaptation do you prefer? | He likes Dune broadly but says he did not care for Part Two because of major story and timeline changes, and he prefers Lynch overall. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=13706" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:26</a> | What are your thoughts on 1 Timothy 2:12? | He says women teaching in church is not the issue as such; he frames the passage around authority, order, and men failing to serve. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=14611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:31</a> | Why is Rehoboam's mother Naamah the Ammonite mentioned twice in 1 Kings 14? | He says the repetition draws attention to her Ammonite identity and its covenantal significance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=14910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:30</a> | Why did Eve add "or touch it" to the divine command in Genesis 3? | He says changing God's wording changes the meaning and can become the first step toward getting around the command. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:18:14</a> | Was Neferneferuaten really Nefertiti? | He says we do not know; Nefertiti, Smenkhkare, or one of Nefertiti's daughters are possible, but the evidence is too limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:22</a> | Was Ramesses II overrated as a pharaoh? | He says yes as a military leader, but praises him as a prolific builder and architect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15694" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:21:34</a> | Which guitarist inspired you to play the SG, and when is the next Velocirabbit album coming? | He says the SG itself appealed for its versatility and affordability, and that a second Velocirabbit album has been started but not finished. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=15977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:26:17</a> | How should Exodus 4:24-26 be understood? | He says the issue is Moses failing to have his son circumcised, so Zipporah's act resolves a covenant violation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=16321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:32:01</a> | Was circumcision only covenantal, did Egyptians practice it, and could it have originated in Egypt? | He says Israelite circumcision was covenantal but had practical effects, Egyptians did practice it, and a sign can be adopted into a covenant without originating there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=16686" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:38:06</a> | How should 1 Corinthians 11:5-10, especially verse 10, be understood? | He says the reference to angels may mean heavenly beings or church messengers, so the verse has more than one possible reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17065" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:44:25</a> | Did Moses write the Genesis patriarch stories, and were they oral tradition or compiled sources? | He thinks Moses likely recorded much of the material, but says the mix of oral and written sources before Sinai is hard to know. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17240" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:47:20</a> | Did Egyptians worship the sun, and why? | Yes. He says sun worship is understandable because the sun is obviously life-giving, dangerous, and overwhelmingly powerful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/oGgvDwIod1U?t=17669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:54:29</a> | What overlooked fact about John the Baptist stands out? | He says John was a Nazirite and highlights the vow's restrictions on hair, ritual purity, and the fruit of the vine. |
